beats.narration: A video borescope spots a flaw ten meters down a pipe. Someone asks the only question that matters — how big is it?

payload.title: HOW BIG IS IT?
payload.sub: Measuring an indication you can't touch, ten meters from your hands.

beats.narration: Through a scope, magnification changes with distance. Move the tip closer and everything grows — the image alone can't tell you size.

payload.heading: A pixel is not a millimeter

beats.narration: So every technique restores scale. Comparison ratios off a known-size reference, shadow projection encodes distance, and stereo tips triangulate depth from two views.
Before you trust any on-screen number, prove it: measure a block of known size, before and after, at a similar distance — and record it.

payload.heading: Prove it before you trust it

beats.narration: Because here's the rule: codes accept demonstrated accuracy, not displayed digits. The screen shows a number — your verification makes it true.

payload.sub: Codes accept demonstrated accuracy, not displayed digits.
